title: Medical device, method, system, controller and computer program product
abstract: A medical device (10) comprising a first circuit (12, C0) for selectively applying power to at least two electrodes applied to a human or animal subject is provided. The first circuit (12, C0) includes: a first switch (S1) electrically connected to a power terminal (PT) and a first electrode terminal (E1), the power terminal (PT) being configured to receive input power from a power source, the first electrode terminal (E1) being configured to be electrically connected to one of said at least two electrodes, the first switch (S1) being arranged in between the power terminal (PT) and the first electrode terminal (E1); a second switch (S2) electrically connected to the power terminal (PT) and a second electrode terminal (E2), the second electrode terminal (E2) being configured to be electrically connected to another one of said at least two electrodes, the second switch (S2) being arranged in between the power terminal (PT) and the second electrode terminal (E2); a third switch (S3) electrically connected to the first electrode terminal (E1) and a ground terminal (GT), the ground terminal (GT) being configured to be electrically connected to a ground, the third switch (S3) being arranged in between the first electrode terminal (E1) and the ground terminal (GT); and a fourth switch (S4) electrically connected to the second electrode terminal (E2) and the ground terminal (GT), the fourth switch (S4) being arranged in between the second electrode terminal (E2) and the ground terminal (GT).
claims:
Claims Applicant: Universitat Heidelberg  "Medical Device, Method, System, Controller and Computer Program Product" Our Ref.: H 3659WO - ds / yma Claims 1 . A medical device (10) comprising a first circuit (12, CO) for selectively applying power to at least two electrodes applied to a human or animal subject, the first circuit (12, CO) including: a first switch (S1 ) electrically connected to a power terminal (PT) and a first electrode terminal (E1 ), the power terminal (PT) being configured to receive input power from a power source/ the first electrode terminal (E1 ) being configured to be electrically connected to one of said at least two electrodes, the first switch (S1 ) being arranged in between the power terminal (PT) and the first electrode terminal (E1 ); a second switch (S2) electrically connected to the power terminal (PT) and a second electrode terminal (E2), the second electrode terminal (E2) being configured to be electrically connected to another one of said at least two electrodes, the second switch (S2) being arranged in between the power terminal (PT) and the second electrode terminal (E2); a third switch (S3) electrically connected to the first electrode terminal (E1 ) and a ground terminal (GT), the ground terminal (GT) being configured to be electrically connected to a ground, the third switch (S3) being arranged in between the first electrode terminal (E1 ) and the ground terminal (GT); and a fourth switch (S4) electrically connected to the second electrode terminal (E2) and the ground terminal (GT), the fourth switch (S4) being arranged in between the second electrode terminal (E2) and the ground terminal (GT). 2. The medical device (10) according to claim 1 , wherein: the first circuit ( 2, CO) further comprises a ground connecting switch (SG) that is electrically connected to the ground terminal (GT) and that is configured to electrically connect or disconnect the ground terminal (GT) to the ground; and the ground connecting switch (SG) may be implemented by two switches (SG ' , SG " ) electrically connected in parallel to the ground terminal (GT). 3. The medical device (10) according to claim 1 or 2, wherein: the first circuit (12, CO) further comprises a first differential amplifier (A1 ), a second differential amplifier (A2), a first measurement terminal (M1 ), a second measurement terminal (M2), a fifth switch (S5), a sixth switch (S6) and a resistance each of the first measurement terminal (M1 ) and the second measurement terminal (M2) is electrically connected to one of two input terminals of the first differential amplifier (A1 ); a first end of the third switch (S3) is electrically connected to the first electrode terminal (E1 ) and a second end of the third switch (S3) is electrically connected to the ground terminal (GT) via the fifth switch (S5) and the resistance (RM); the second end of the third switch (S3) is further electrically connected to the first measurement terminal (M1 ); a first end of the fourth switch (S4) is electrically connected to the second electrode terminal (E2) and a second end of the fourth switch (S4) is electrically connected to the ground terminal (GT) via the sixth switch (S6) and the resistance (RM); the second end of the fourth switch (S4) is further electrically connected to the second measurement terminal (M2); a first end of the fifth switch (S5) is electrically connected to the second end of the third switch (S3) and to the first measurement terminal (M1 ); a second end of the fifth switch (S5) is electrically connected to a first end of the resistance (RM); a first end of the sixth switch (S6) is electrically connected to the second end of the fourth switch (S4) and to the second measurement terminal (M2); a second end of the sixth switch (S6) is electrically connected to the first end of the resistance (RM); a second end of the resistance (RM) is electrically connected to the ground terminal (GT); and each of the first and second ends of the resistance (RM) is electrically connected to one of two input terminals of the second differential amplifier (A2). 4. The medical device (10) according to claim 1 or 2, wherein: 

 the first circuit (12, CO) further comprises a first differential amplifier (A1 ), a second differential amplifier (A2), a circuit breaker (CB), a fifth switch (S5), a sixth switch (S6) and a resistance (RM); each of the first and second electrode terminals (E1 , E2) is electrically connected to one of two input terminals of the first differential amplifier (A1 ) via the circuit breaker (CB), the circuit breaker (CB) being configured to electrically disconnect the first differential amplifier (A1 ) from the first and second electrode terminals (E1 , E2) in case a voltage applied between the first and second electrode terminals (E1 , E2) is higher than a specified threshold value; a first end of the third switch (S3) is electrically connected to the first electrode terminal (E1 ) and a second end of the third switch (S3) is electrically connected to the ground terminal (GT) via the fifth switch (S5) and the resistance (RM); a first end of the fourth switch (S4) is electrically connected to the second electrode terminal (E2) and a second end of the fourth switch (S4) is electrically connected to the ground terminal (GT) via the sixth switch (S6) and the resistance (RM); the second end of the third switch (S3) and the second end of the fourth switch (S4) are electrically connected to each other; a first end of the fifth switch (S5) is electrically connected to the second end of the third switch (S3) and a second end of the fifth switch (S5) is electrically connected to a first end of the resistance (RM); a first end of the sixth switch (S6) is electrically connected to the second end of the fourth switch (S4) and a second end of the sixth switch (S6) is electrically connected to the first end of the resistance (RM); a second end of the resistance (RM) is electrically connected to the ground terminal (GT); and each of the first and second ends of the resistance (RM) is electrically connected to one of two input terminals of the second differential amplifier (A2). 5. The medical device (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, further comprising at least one second circuit (12, C1 ), wherein: said at least one second circuit (12, C1 ) has the features of the first circuit ( 2, CO); the first and second electrode terminals (E3, E4) of said at least one second 

 circuit (12, C1 ) are configured to be electrically connected to electrodes that are different from the electrodes to be connected to the first and second electrode terminals (E1 , E2) of the first circuit (12, CO); the first circuit (12, CO) and said at least one second circuit (12, C1 ) may be configured to receive input power from different power sources; and the first circuit (12, CO) and said at least one second circuit (12, C1 ) may be galvanically isolated from one another either by true galvanic isolation or virtual galvanic isolation. 6. A method performed by a controller (20) configured to control the medical device (10) according to any one claims 1 to 4, the method comprising controlling the medical device (10) to operate, in accordance with a setting made by a user, in one of the following modes: a stimulation mode in which a part of the human or animal subject is electrically stimulated by two electrodes connected to the first and second electrode terminals (E1 , E2), wherein the controller (20) controls the medical device (10) to set: the first and fourth switches (S1 , S4) to a closed state and the second and third switches (S2, S3) to an open state; or the first and fourth switches (S1 , S4) to the open state and the second and third switches (S2, S3) to the closed state; an inactive mode wherein the controller (20) controls the medical device (10) to set the first, second, third and fourth switches (S1 , S2, S3, S4) to the open state; a short circuit mode wherein the controller (20) controls the medical device (10) to set the first and second switches (S1 , S2) to the open state and the third and fourth switches (S3, S4) to the closed state. 7. The method according to claim 6, wherein: the controller (20) is configured to control the medical device (10) of claim 2; in the stimulation mode and the inactive mode, the controller (20) controls the medical device (10) to set the ground connecting switch (SG) to the closed state; and in the short circuit mode, the controller (20) controls the medical device (10) to set the ground connecting switch (SG) to the open state. 8. The method according to claim 6 or 7, wherein: 

 the controller (20) is configured to control the medical device (10) of claim 3; in the inactive mode, the controller (20) controls the medical device (10) to set the fifth and sixth switches (S5, S6) to the closed state while setting the first, second, third and fourth switches (S1 , S2, S3, S4) to the open state; in the stimulation mode, the controller (20) controls the medical device (10) to set the fifth and sixth switches (S5, S6) to the closed state; in the short circuit mode, the controller (20) controls the medical device (10) to set the first and second switches (S1 , S2) to the open state and the third, fourth, fifth and sixth switches (S3, S4, S5, S6) to the closed state; the controller (20) is further configured to control the medical device (10) to operate, in accordance with the setting by the user, in the stimulation mode, the inactive mode, the short circuit mode, a signal measurement mode or an impedance measurement mode, wherein in the signal measurement mode, the controller (20) controls the medical device (10) to: set the first, second, fifth and sixth switches (S1 , S2, S5, S6) to the open state and the third and fourth switches (S3, S4) to the closed state; and determine a signal between the first and second electrode terminals (El, E2) using an output from the first differential amplifier (A1 ); and in the impedance measurement mode, the controller (20) controls the medical device (10) to: set the third and fourth switches (S3, S4) to the closed state while setting: the first and sixth switches (S1 , S6) to the closed state and the second and fifth switches (S2, S5) to the open state; or the first and sixth switches (S1 , S6) to the open state and the second and fifth switches (S2, S5) to the closed state; and determine an impedance between the first and second electrode terminals (E1 , E2) using outputs from the first and second differential amplifiers (A1 , A2). 9. The method according to claim 6 or 7, wherein: the controller (20) is configured to control the medical device (10) of claim 4; in the stimulation mode, the controller (20) controls the medical device (10) to: 

 set one or both of the fifth and sixth switches (S5, S6) to the closed state; and set the circuit breaker (CB) to the open state; in the inactive mode, the controller (20) controls the medical device (10) to set the fifth and sixth switches (S5, S6) to the closed state and the circuit breaker (CB) to the open state; in the short circuit mode, the controller (20) controls the medical device (10) to set the fifth and sixth switches (S5, S6) to the open state and the circuit breaker (CB) to the open state; and the controller (20) is configured to control the medical device (10) to operate, in accordance with the setting by the user, in the stimulation mode, the inactive mode, the short circuit mode or a signal measurement mode, wherein, in the signal measurement mode, the controller controls the medical device to: set the first, second, third and fourth switches (S1 , S2, S3, S4) to the open state; set the fifth and sixth switches (S5, S6) to the closed state; set the circuit breaker (CB) to the closed state; and determine a signal between the first and second electrode terminals (E1 , E2) using an output from the first differential amplifier (A1 ). 10. A method performed by a controller (20) configured to control the medical device (10) according to claim 5, the method comprising: controlling the medical device (10) to operate in a stimulation mode in case of electrically stimulating a part of the human or animal subject by a pair of electrodes connected to a pair of electrode terminals selected from the first and second electrode terminals (E1 , E2, E3, E4) in the first and second circuits (12, CO, C1 ), wherein, in the stimulation mode, the controller (20) controls the medical device (10) to set each switch in the first and second circuits (12, CO, C1 ) to the closed state or the open state so as to: electrically connect one of the selected pair of electrode terminals to the power terminal (PT) of the circuit comprising the one of the selected pair of electrode terminals; electrically connect another one of the selected pair of electrode terminals to the ground terminal (GT) of the circuit comprising the other one of the 

 selected pair of electrode terminals; and electrically disconnect the first and second electrode terminals other than the selected pair of electrode terminals from the ground terminal (GT) and from the power terminal (PT). 1 1 . The method according to claim 10, wherein: the controller (20) is configured to control the medical device (10) having the features of claims 3 and 5; in the stimulation mode, the controller (20) controls the medical device to set the fifth and sixth switches (S5, S6) of the first and second circuits (12, CO, C1 ) to the closed state; the controller (20) is further configured to control the medical device (10) to operate in an impedance measurement mode in case of measuring an impedance between a pair of electrode terminals selected from the first and second electrode terminals (E1 , E2, E3, E4) in the first and second circuits (12, CO, C1 ), wherein, in the impedance measurement mode, the controller (20) controls the medical device (10) to: when the selected pair of electrode terminals are comprised in a same circuit, set the third and fourth switches (S3, S4) of that circuit to the closed state while setting: the first and sixth switches (S1 , S6) of that circuit to the closed state and the second and fifth switches (S2, S5) of that circuit to the open state; or the first and sixth switches (S1 , S6) of that circuit to the open state and the second and fifth switches (S2, S5) of that circuit to the closed state; and set the first and second switches (S1 , S2) of the first or second circuit that does not comprise the selected pair of electrode terminals to the open state; and when each one of the selected pair of electrode terminals are comprised in a different circuit, set each switch of the first and second circuits (12, CO, C1 ) to the closed state or the open state so as to: electrically connect one of the selected pair of electrode terminals to the power terminal (PT) and the first or second measurement terminal of the circuit comprising the one of the selected pair of electrode terminals; electrically connect, to the ground terminal (GT) via the resistance (RM), the first or second measurement terminal that is comprised in the circuit comprising the one of the selected pair of electrode terminals and that is not electrically connected 

 to the one of the selected pair of electrode terminals; electrically connect another one of the selected pair of electrode terminals to the first or second measurement terminal of the circuit comprising the other one of the selected pair of electrode terminals; electrically connect, to the ground terminal (GT) via the resistance (RM), the first and second measurement terminals of the circuit comprising the other one of the selected pair of electrode terminals; and electrically disconnect the first and second electrode terminals other than the selected pair of electrode terminals from the ground terminal (GT) and from the power terminal (PT). 12. A computer program product comprising computer-readable instructions that, when loaded and run on a computer, cause the computer to perform the method according to any one of claims 6 to 1 1 . 13. A controller (20) configured to perform the method according to any one of claims 6 to 1 1 . 14. A system (1 ) comprising: the medical device (10) according to any one of claims 1 to 5; and the controller (20) according to claim 13. 15. The system (1 ) according to claim 14, further comprising: a floating current source as the power source that supplies the input power to the power terminal (PT) of the medical device (10).
